result.summary: The page is generally recognizable, but several elements appear clipped or inconsistent on the mobile viewport. The main visual problems are horizontal content overflowing/cropping, the fixed bottom navigation covering game tiles, and a mismatched active navigation state.

issues.description: Game tiles in the content sections are clipped on the right edge of the screen. The third column is only partially visible, making the grid look wider than the viewport or like unintended horizontal overflow.
issues.suggestion: Adjust the mobile layout to fit the viewport, such as using a 2-column grid, adding proper horizontal carousel behavior with clear scroll affordance, or preventing page-level horizontal overflow.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation overlaps the game content. The lower row of game cards is partially hidden behind the bottom nav bar.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding or safe-area spacing to the scrollable content so the final cards remain fully visible above the fixed navigation.

issues.description: The bottom navigation highlights “POKER” as the active tab, but the visible page content is casino game content such as BetRivers Exclusive Games and Top Played New Games. This makes the selected state look incorrect.
issues.suggestion: Update the active tab to match the current page, likely “CASINO,” or ensure the displayed content matches the selected Poker tab.

issues.description: The top category navigation is clipped on the right, cutting off the “Hot Games” item. If this is intended to be horizontally scrollable, the visual affordance is unclear.
issues.suggestion: Provide clearer horizontal scrolling behavior, add end padding, or resize category pills so visible items are not awkwardly truncated.
